title: Root packet docs still carry post-merge identity drift outside the strategy artifact
summary: REQ-014 and packet metadata/evidence still reference legacy 010-memory-indexer-invariants while the parent metadata now points to 005-memory-indexer-invariants.

title: Feature catalog entry is mostly current but points at stale packet and ADR identifiers
summary: The catalog describes index-scope invariants, but labels the packet as 026/010/002, cites README exclusion as ADR-005 instead of ADR-006, and omits the cleanup-specific action/helper surface.

title: Manual playbook covers broad save/index scenario but misses first-class adversarial scenarios
summary: The playbook has one broad context-save scenario but no first-class cleanup apply, checkpoint restore, walker DoS, or non-save constitutional promotion bypass scenarios.

title: Runtime trace comments point ADR readers at the wrong packet
summary: Implementation comments cite packet 026/011 even though the current packet is 026/.../005-memory-indexer-invariants.
REQ-001..REQ-016 mostly map to implementation, but REQ-014 contradicts current parent metadata and REQ-015 remains blocked by live MCP rescan.
61 checked CHK items: 1 concrete file-line pass, 40 partial file-only citations, 20 narrative/command-output failures; CHK-T15 blocked with accurate rationale.
Catalog contains the index-scope capability but has stale packet/ADR identifiers and incomplete governance-audit helper/action coverage.
Broad context-save scenario exists, but cleanup apply, checkpoint restore, walker DoS, and non-save promotion bypass scenarios are not first-class playbook entries.
